suppress.notes: spring-security-core-6.5.9.jar: CVE-2018-1258 only applies when used with spring-framework 5.0.5, which is not in this project. Added: 2026-04-17

spring-security-crypto-6.5.9.jar: CVE-2018-1258 only applies when used with spring-framework 5.0.5, which is not in this project. The NVD CPE cpe:/a:pivotal_software:spring_security matches all spring-security artifacts including crypto, but the vulnerability is context-specific to framework 5.0.5. Added: 2026-04-20

spring-security-crypto-6.5.10.jar: CVE-2018-1258 only applies when used with spring-framework 5.0.5, which is not in this project. The NVD CPE cpe:/a:pivotal_software:spring_security matches all spring-security artifacts including crypto, but the vulnerability is context-specific to framework 5.0.5. Added: 2026-04-20
spring-security-core-6.5.10.jar: CVE-2018-1258 only applies when used with spring-framework 5.0.5, which is not in this project. Added: 2026-04-20

protobuf-java (all versions): the NVD CPE cpe:/a:google:protobuf is a cross-language CPE that NVD assigns to every Google Protobuf CVE regardless of which language binding is actually affected. This causes Python-only and C++-only CVEs to incorrectly match the Java protobuf-java artifact. The originating example is CVE-2026-0994, which is a bug in the Python protobuf library's json_format.ParseDict() function (a recursion-depth counter bug in json_format.py) — code that does not exist in the Java implementation. Fix versions cited there (5.29.6, 6.33.5) are Python package versions.
This entry suppresses the entire cpe:/a:google:protobuf CPE association for all versions of pkg:maven/com.google.protobuf/protobuf-java, so future Python-only or C++-only CVEs under the same CPE do not require a new suppression entry per version bump. A genuine Java-implementation CVE would be filed under a Java-specific CPE (e.g. cpe:/a:google:protobuf-java) and would not be hidden by this rule. Added: 2026-05-22
